description:
## Goal

Replace every `eprintln!`/`format!()`/`Result<_, String>` error in the pipeline with a `Diagnostic` constructed against the .20.5 type and rendered via .20.6. Drive every error through one common output path so the renderer is the *only* thing writing user-facing error text.

## Dependencies

- Requires .20.1 (Span), .20.2 (AST spans), .20.3 (resolve/macro spans), .20.4 (ir_lower spans), .20.5 (Diagnostic), .20.6 (Renderer).

## The punch-list

This is the audit-trail of every error site from the .20 epic's investigation, with the migration target for each.

### Lexer (`src/lexer.rs`)

- `LexError { msg, line, col }` becomes `Diagnostic` with primary span at the error position. Codes:
  - `lex/unterminated-string` (when string literal hits EOF without close-quote)
  - `lex/unterminated-bitstring` (`<<` without `>>`)
  - `lex/invalid-char` (unknown lead byte)
  - `lex/invalid-number` (malformed int/float literal)
  - `lex/invalid-escape` (bad `\` in string)
- Lexer returns `Result<Vec<Token>, Diagnostics>` instead of `Result<_, LexError>`.

### Parser (`src/parser.rs`)

- `ParseError` becomes `Diagnostic`. Primary span = the unexpected/missing token's span.
- Every `self.err("‚Ä¶")` becomes a constructor for a specific code. Audit the existing `self.err(‚Ä¶)` call sites and choose the right code per message.
- "expected `fn`, `defmacro`, ‚Ä¶" type errors ‚Üí `parse/expected-token`; secondary span on the surrounding construct if useful (e.g. for an unterminated `do`-block, secondary at the `do`).
- Special cases:
  - `parse/duplicate-moduledoc` (existing message at `parser.rs` `duplicate @moduledoc`) ‚Äî primary at the second `@moduledoc`, secondary at the first.
  - `parse/duplicate-doc` ‚Äî same idea.
  - `parse/dangling-doc` ‚Äî `@doc` not followed by a fn.
  - `parse/unknown-attribute` ‚Äî `@foo` where foo isn't doc/moduledoc.
  - `parse/macro-call-shape` ‚Äî "expected a macro call at item position" / "item-level call must have a bare-name callee".

### Resolve (`src/resolve.rs`)

- Return type becomes `Result<Program, Diagnostics>`.
- Sites:
  - `Item::Alias` / `Item::Import` outside a module (`resolve.rs:58-63`) ‚Üí `resolve/alias-outside-module` / `resolve/import-outside-module`; primary at the alias/import span.
  - Future: unknown module references ‚Äî `resolve/unknown-module`; primary at the use site, secondary at any matching close-name.

### Macros (`src/macros.rs`)

- Return type becomes `Result<(), Diagnostics>`.
- Sites:
  - "item-level call `{}(...)` is not a defmacro" (`macros.rs:79`) ‚Üí `macro/not-a-defmacro`. Primary at the call expression's span.
  - "macro `{}` arg reification" (`macros.rs:85`) ‚Üí `macro/arg-reification-failed`. Primary at the call; secondary at the offending arg if `expr_to_value` can return position info (it can't today ‚Äî extend its error to carry an index, then map to args[i].span).
  - "macro `{}` body" (`macros.rs:91`) ‚Üí `macro/body-failed`. Primary at the call; the inner runtime error becomes a note. (Later iteration: if the body error has a span ‚Äî once interp errors get spans ‚Äî that's the primary instead.)
  - "macro `{}` return" / "macro `{}` return decode" (`macros.rs:93, 255`) ‚Üí `macro/return-decode-failed`.
  - "MAX_EXPANSION_DEPTH exceeded" (`macros.rs:225`) ‚Üí `macro/expansion-loop`. Primary at the call where the loop was detected; help text suggests checking for a missing `unquote` or self-call.
  - "unknown item tag :{}" (`macros.rs:153`) / "expected item tag atom" (`macros.rs:137`) ‚Üí `macro/bad-item-shape`. Primary at the macro call.
  - "expand_with: pre-resolution Item reached macro expander" (`macros.rs:192`) ‚Üí internal-error path; render but flag as a compiler bug (`internal/post-resolution-leftover`).

### ir_lower (`src/ir_lower.rs`)

- `LowerError` becomes `Diagnostic`. Primary span = the offending `Spanned<Expr>` or `Spanned<Pattern>` (.20.2 supplied them).
- `LowerError::Unsupported` (`ir_lower.rs:31`) ‚Üí `lower/unsupported`. Note text from the existing message.
- `LowerError::Unbound` ‚Üí `lower/unbound`. Primary at the use site. Future enhancement: search for similar in-scope names for a `did you mean` help.
- `LowerError::ArityMismatch` ‚Üí `lower/arity-mismatch`. Primary at the call; secondary at the fn definition (`fn_span` from .20.4 by callee name).
- `LowerError::PostExpansionNode` ‚Üí internal-error path; `internal/post-expansion-leftover`.

### ir_typer (`src/ir_typer.rs`)

- Already produces structured diagnostics post-.24.6. After .20.5 the `ir_typer::Diagnostic` was replaced; this ticket finishes the migration:
  - Replace the `format!("‚Ä¶bb{} v{}‚Ä¶", b.id.0, v.0)` message construction (`ir_typer.rs:598-602`) with proper Span lookup from `Module.source.term_span` for the If terminator, plus the offending Var's `source.var_name[v.0]` (rendered as backtick-quoted name).
  - Primary span: the offending branch's `Term::If` span (the predicate or the branch keyword ‚Äî pick the most localized).
  - Note text: the actual type rendering, from .20.8.

### ir_codegen (`src/ir_codegen.rs`)

- The `compile` function's `Result<CompiledModule, String>` becomes `Result<CompiledModule, Diagnostics>`.
- Existing `panic!` sites:
  - `ir_codegen.rs:113` "trampoline exceeded N iterations" ‚Äî this is a **runtime** error, not a compile error. Stays as a panic for now (process-model handling in .19 will replace). Note in the ticket that future work makes this a `runtime/trampoline-overflow` diagnostic with a fz-frame trace.
  - `ir_codegen.rs:121` "no fn for schema_id" ‚Äî compiler invariant; if it ever fires it's a bug. Convert to `internal/schema-missing` diagnostic (still fatal, but rendered).
- Other `.expect()` / `.unwrap()` in codegen are mostly tag/representation invariants (`v.unbox_int().unwrap()` etc.). Leave as-is unless they're plausibly user-facing.

### Interp / runtime (`src/eval.rs`)

- `Interp::call_named` returns `Result<Value, String>`. Migration is significant because every runtime builtin error today is a `format!()` string.
- v1 deliverable: the *outer* error becomes a `Diagnostic` at the call's span (which the interp would need to track ‚Äî currently it doesn't, since it walks the *legacy AST path*, not the IR).
- **Cleanly out of scope for .20.7:** wiring runtime errors with a backtrace through the *IR-execution* path requires the .11.x runtime work to surface frame info. v1 of .20.7 keeps interp errors as best-effort `runtime/<kind>` codes with the *macro-expansion call span* (the top-level call into the macro/test that triggered it) as primary, no IR-level backtrace. Note in the ticket: "richer runtime traces are a .19-era follow-up".

### Test runner (`src/test_runner.rs`)

- `TestRunError(String)` (`test_runner.rs:42-48`) becomes a `Diagnostics`. Each failing test produces a `runtime/assertion-failed` diagnostic; the runner aggregates them and the renderer emits a per-failure block.
- The PRELUDE concat issue (`test_runner.rs:58`) is handled by .20.1's multi-file SourceMap registration: prelude as `FileId(0)`, user file as `FileId(1)`. The renderer naturally shows the user's file path on assertion failures, not "line 7" referring to PRELUDE-shifted lines.

### REPL (`src/repl.rs`)

- `Outcome::Err(String)` (`repl.rs:54, 72, 93, ‚Ä¶`) becomes `Outcome::Err(Diagnostics)`.
- REPL renders via the same `Renderer`, but with a synthetic file id per input line (so spans within an input line render with `repl:1:5` style locations). REPL line numbers carry across history; the SourceMap accumulates per input.

### Driver (`src/main.rs`)

- Every `eprintln!` and `std::process::exit(1)` after a stage failure is removed.
- Replaced with `renderer.emit_all(&diags, &mut io::stderr())?` followed by `process::exit(1)` if `diags.has_errors()`.
- Warnings from a successful compile (e.g. ir_typer's UnreachableArm) are rendered before `cm.run(main_fn)` exits.
- Test-runner subcommand and REPL go through the same `Renderer`.

## Single render path

Define `src/diag/driver.rs` (or just `src/diag.rs` if everything fits) with:

```rust
pub fn render_to_stderr(sm: &SourceMap, diags: &Diagnostics) {
    let renderer = Renderer::new(sm);
    let _ = renderer.emit_all(diags, &mut std::io::stderr().lock());
}
```

Every error sink in `main.rs`, `repl.rs`, `test_runner.rs` calls `render_to_stderr`. No `eprintln!("error: ‚Ä¶")` anywhere else.

## Tests

This is largely covered by tests at each prior level (.20.1-.20.6). The new tests here are end-to-end:

- **`fz run` on a file with a lex error**: golden output matches expected rendered text.
- **`fz run` on a file with an unbound name**: golden output.
- **`fz run` on a file with a macro expansion error**: golden output, including the `expanded from` trailer.
- **`fz test` on a file with a failing assertion**: per-test rendered block.
- **`fz test` on `fixtures/modules.fz`**: clean run, no diagnostics emitted.

Golden files live in `tests/diag/golden/`. The driver writes to stderr, tests capture and compare. NO_COLOR is set in the test env so escapes don't pollute golden files.

## Deliverables

- All error sites listed above migrated.
- `src/diag/driver.rs` rendering helper.
- `main.rs` and `repl.rs` and `test_runner.rs` simplified to render-once-and-exit pattern.
- Golden tests under `tests/diag/golden/`.

## Non-goals (deferred)

- Interp errors with full IR-frame backtraces (waits for .19 process work).
- Runtime panics in ir_codegen-generated code (trampoline overflow stays a panic).
- IDE / JSON output.
- `fz check` subcommand that emits diagnostics without running. Worth a follow-up ticket post-.20.
title: Wire all error sites: lex/parse/resolve/macros/lower/type/codegen/runtime
